name: Sidney Bechet
abstract: Sidney Joseph Bechet (May 14, 1897 – May 14, 1959) was an American jazz saxophonist, clarinetist, and composer. He was one of the first important soloists in jazz, and first recorded several months before trumpeter Louis Armstrong.

works_created.t: When the Sun Sets Down South
The Hill on the Delta (La Colline du Delta)

about_quotes.speaker: Clyde Kerr Jr.
about_quotes.evidence: George Baquet was a very famous clarinet player who played in the twenties and the thirties, in fact he was one of the teachers of a great saxophone player and a clarinet player from New Orleans called Sidney Bechet. So my great uncle.
about_quotes.doc_title: Clyde Kerr Jr. interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rome, New York, August 22, 2006

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: And I’m thinking of people like Benny Carter, Chu Berry, Fletcher Henderson, all of the great big bands, even all of the soloists in the Ellington bands at the time. Sidney Bechet. Those were incredible improvisers.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Wilson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 25, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Cliff Leeman
about_quotes.evidence: Sidney asked if I'd go in with him with a quartet... The band was Vic Dickson, Kenny Kersey and myself... I imagine the way I played then to accompany Bechet

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: I listened to Sidney Bechet and Muggsy Spanier and Budd Freeman. ... I remember hearing . . . hearing Sidney Bechet playing “Shake It But Don’t Break It”
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Schuller Gunther
about_quotes.evidence: whether it’s Freddie Keppard or it’s Louis Armstrong or Sidney Bechet or whoever it is. That was then picked up by mostly white musicians...
about_quotes.doc_title: Schuller Gunther — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Buster Smith
about_quotes.evidence: The man I always admired as playing the horn was Sidney Bechet. ... I tried to copy behind him. I'd buy all his records and listen to him.
